body.title: Granny: watchdog to reset stale in_progress tickets back to sprint
body.result: _reset_stale_inprogress() using reset --timeout; circuit breaker caps retries at 3; 4 tests
body.intention: I intend that stale in_progress claude tickets are automatically reset using the existing --timeout circuit breaker, so runaway retry loops self-halt after 3 attempts rather than burning unlimited tokens.
body.description:
Tickets stuck at status=in_progress with worker IN ('claude','cc') block _cc0_busy() from ever clearing, preventing Granny from dispatching new work. Today 8 tickets were stuck this way after sprint sessions were interrupted. _escalate_stale_dispatched() handles dispatched tickets but nothing handles in_progress.

Fix: add _reset_stale_inprogress() in daemon.py — query status='in_progress' AND worker IN ('claude','cc') AND updated_at < now() - interval '2 hours', then for each: call cc_queue.py reset --timeout {ticket_id}. The --timeout flag increments a reset counter and automatically holds the ticket at 3 resets, capping the retry-loop blast radius at ~3 sprint attempts (~450k tokens worst case). Call it at the top of run_once() alongside _escalate_stale_dispatched().

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; test the DB query path with a mock
**Scope boundary:** New function _reset_stale_inprogress() + one call site in run_once(). No changes to _escalate_stale_dispatched() or dispatch logic. Out of scope: the reset --timeout counter itself (already in cc_queue.py).
**Completion criteria:** Given a stale in_progress claude ticket in the mock DB, the function calls cc_queue.py reset --timeout {ticket_id} (not setstatus sprint). Test: mock psycopg2.connect returns the stale row; assert subprocess call includes 'reset' and '--timeout'. After 3 resets, cc_queue.py holds the ticket — verify by checking status=hold after 3 calls.
